ImageDescription.value: Augustus Newton Whiting (9/30/1836-12/22/1903) founded St. Phillip's Episcopal Church in 1894, the first episcopal church for African Americans in Columbus. He was the son of Isaac Newton, a bookseller; and Orrel Kilbourne Newton, the daughter of James Kilbourne, who founded Worthington, Ohio. Whiting's home at 66 South 3rd Street was built by his father in 1839 and demolished on March 21, 1930. Whiting's obituary appears in the Columbus Dispatch, 12/22/1903, page 8. His wife was Ellen Gilbert.
